blocks.text:
# SPRING GLOW (SGX) PROGRAM — SPEND & RISK AUDIT MEMO

**Scope:** Campaign, Finance, Legal, HR, Operations, IT records tagged to "Spring Glow Campaign Execution (SGX)" / the GlowSerum/GlowLuxe launch.

**Preliminary data-quality note (applies to every section below):** The corpus contains numerous near-duplicate document pairs (a base file and a "_v1" file) that are otherwise identical in wording but differ in embedded dates, invoice numbers, or line-item dollar figures. This pattern recurs across Campaign, Legal, Finance, and IT records (e.g., `invoice_0203_288.md`/`invoice_0203_288_v1.txt`, `invoice_0091_116.md`/`_v1`, `budget_1720_421.txt`/`.md`/`_v1.md`, `budget_0355_340.txt`/`_v1.md`, `ticket_0949_378.txt`/`_v1.md`). This materially undermines confidence in any single "final" number and is treated as a standing audit finding, not just noise.

---

## 1. TOTAL PROGRAM COST — ALL DEPARTMENTS

No single document in the corpus presents a validated, cross-department, Finance-certified grand total. Every "total" document located was authored by **Campaign Department staff**, not Finance:

| Candidate Total | Source | Prepared By | Scope stated |
|---|---|---|---|
| $54,200 | `spring_glow_budget.csv` (id 343) | unspecified (Campaign) | Media $25,000 + Production $15,000 + Agency $8,000 + Talent $5,000 + Printing $1,200. Creative-line-items only — **no staffing, legal, IT, or logistics.** |
| $435,000 | `budget_1720_421.txt`/`.md`/`_v1.md` (ids 364, 103, 350) | Sarah Chen, prepared 2026-01-28 | Labeled "Total Budget Allocated," breaks out Campaign Management salaries (~$120–121k), Content Creation ($45–60k), Production/Melbournescope Studios $70k, Digital Advertising $60k, on-site activation + contingency. |
| ≥$283,333 (incomplete) | `budget_1300_924.md`/`.csv`/`_v1.csv` (ids 345, 1002, 180, 352) | unspecified | SouthCoast Logistics (Campaign Production) $158,333 + Southern Logistics Pty Ltd (Event Coordination) $80,000 + CRM Integration $45,000 + an unseen Staffing/creative-team line. |
| $439,833 (computed) | `budget_1750_589.md`/`.csv`/`_v1.csv` (ids 346, 1181, 351) | unspecified | Skyline Analytics (Campaign Mgmt/Data Analytics) $158,333 + EcoPackaging Australia (Sustainable Packaging) $8,500 + Campaign Material Distribution $15,000 + CRM System Upgrade $48,000 + Dept. Staff Salaries $210,000. (Card summary separately references a $95,000 "advertising" figure that could not be confirmed as an additional row — if additive, total is $534,833.) |
| $525,000 budgeted / $525,600 actual (+0.1%) | `budget_0355_340.txt` (127) / `_v1.md` (367) | Quinn Reyes, Media Planner, Campaign Dept. | Explicitly named categories: Creative Production, Media Buying, Market Research, and (in the _v1 only) "Legal & Compliance" — the only version that names Legal spend as an included category. |

**Reconciliation / authoritative source:** I designate **$435,000 (`budget_1720_421` family)** as the most defensible "Campaign-side" baseline, because it is the only figure that (a) is explicitly labeled as a "Total Budget Allocated" header rather than a derived sum, (b) survived unchanged across three independently-filed renditions (.txt, .md, .md v1) even though their internal sub-allocations drifted ($120k vs $121k salaries; $45k vs $60k Content Creation), and (c) is the most recently dated (2026-01-28, post a 2025-12-10 vendor-contract review).

However, **this figure does not represent true all-departments cost.** None of its named vendors overlap with the vendors billing Legal, Finance, or Operations for SGX (Red Centre Legal, Dingo Consulting, NorthPac Legal, SouthCoast Logistics, Southern Logistics Pty Ltd — see §3). Layering in the clearly incremental Legal invoices not reflected in the $435,000 figure — Red Centre Legal $8,450 + Dingo Consulting $7,200 (`invoice_0203_288.md`, id 260) + NorthPac Legal $12,450 (`invoice_0091_116.md`, id 491) — raises the floor to **≈$463,100**. If the Finance/Operations-side $158,333 logistics/analytics line and $210,000 salary line (`budget_1750_589`) are genuinely additive rather than restatements of Campaign's own $121,000 salary and $158,333-tagged line, true cost could run as high as **~$534,833–$550,000**.

**Bottom line:** Total documented SGX program cost cannot be reconciled to one number with the available records. Best-supported range: **$463,000 (conservative floor) to ~$535,000 (if Finance/Ops lines are additive)**, against a low-end creative-only figure of $54,200 that materially understates true cost, exactly as flagged in the assignment.

---

## 2. RISK REGISTER

| # | Item | Dept | Amount | Status | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Red Centre Legal invoice (legal review/compliance audit) | Legal | $8,450.00 | Amount Due (unpaid) | `invoice_0203_288.md`/`_v1` (260/495) |
| 2 | Dingo Consulting invoice (market research/audience segmentation) | Legal | $7,200.00 | Amount Due (unpaid) | `invoice_0203_288.md`/`_v1` (260/495) |
| 3 | NorthPac Legal invoice (legal review + IT infrastructure setup) | Legal | $12,450.00 | Amount Due (unpaid) | `invoice_0091_116.md`/`_v1` (491/490) |
| 4 | NorthPac Legal line item (Campaign creative-invoice ledger) | Campaign | $7,200.00 | Unclear/conflicts with #3 (same vendor, different amount — see §3) | `budget_0446_702.md` (359) |
| 5 | Aurora Research Pty Ltd invoice | Finance | $5,750.00 | Pending, due 2025-10-30 | `invoice_1347_984.csv`/`_v1` (89/1303) |
| 6 | EcoPackaging Australia invoice | Finance | $3,200.00 | Pending, due 2025-10-30 | `invoice_1347_984.csv`/`_v1` (89/1303) |
| 7 | Red Centre Legal invoice (second stream) | Finance | $4,850.00 | Pending, due 2025-10-30 | `invoice_1347_984.csv`/`_v1` (89/1303) |
| 8 | Aussie Web Solutions / Red Centre Legal invoices | Finance | $1,750.00–$6,200.00 | Pending Approval / Draft / Unpaid (mixed) | `invoice_0329_735.csv`/`.md`/`_v1` (1180/257/497) |
| 9 | Budget lines flagged "Pending approval" (CRM System Upgrade $48,000 and/or other Marketing/Operations/Technology/Personnel lines) | Finance/Ops/IT | up to $48,000 | Pending approval | `budget_1750_589.md` (346) |
| 10 | TVC assets on hold — color-gradient discrepancy between packshot (Skyline Analytics) and design brief | Campaign/IT | Not costed | Hold — schedule/delivery risk | `memo_0764_205.txt`/`_v1` (727/401) |
| 11 | Staffing count discrepancies: Operations 14 reported vs 16 scheduled; Accounting 9 vs 12 | HR/Legal/Ops | Not costed (payroll-integrity risk) | Under review; Wattle Accounting & Red Centre Legal engaged | `memo_0533_432.md`/`_v1` (798/791) |
| 12 | Open/unresolved IT tickets tied to SGX impact analysis (LMS login failures #1025, CRM/marketing-dashboard sync error #1027) | IT | Not costed | In Progress/Open | `ticket_0949_378.txt`/`_v1.md` (223/212) |
| 13 | GlowSerum campaign website slow load times (IT-2025-008); marketing network latency (IT-2025-005) | IT | Not costed | Open | `ticket_1999_202.md`/`_v1.csv` (214/207) |
| 14 | Vendor checklists for SGX vendors (Tasmanian IT, GoldCoast Marketing) not yet finalized | Legal | Not costed | Pending finalization | `status_report_1464_849.md`/`_v1` (181/975) |

---

## 3. VENDOR CROSS-REFERENCE

**Campaign's own named SGX vendor set** (derived from Campaign-authored documents — `spring_glow_budget.csv`, `budget_1720_421` family, `budget_0446_702.md`, `meeting_notes_1150_752.md`, `memo_0764_205.txt`): **Melbournescope Studios, Kangaroo Designs, NorthPac Legal, Aurora Research Pty Ltd, Skyline Analytics.**

**Billed-amount conflicts (same vendor, different figures across departments):**
- **NorthPac Legal:** $7,200 in Campaign's invoice ledger (`budget_0446_702.md`, 359) vs. **$12,450** in Legal's dedicated invoice (`invoice_0091_116.md`, 491/490) — same vendor, same SGX project, ~73% discrepancy.
- **"$158,333" line:** attributed to **SouthCoast Logistics** (Campaign Production) in `budget_1300_924` (345/1002) vs. attributed to **Skyline Analytics** (Campaign Management/Data Analytics) in `budget_1750_589` (346/1181) — identical dollar figure assigned to two different vendor names for what appears to be the same underlying cost. High-priority reconciliation item; also raises risk of duplicate booking.
- **CRM cost:** $45,000 "CRM Integration" (`budget_1300_924`) vs. $48,000 "CRM System Upgrade" (`budget_1750_589`) — same scope, different vendor unnamed, different amount.
- **EcoPackaging Australia:** $8,500 budget allocation (`budget_1750_589`) vs. $3,200 single invoice (`invoice_1347_984`) — not necessarily contradictory (could be partial billing) but unreconciled.

**Naming-similarity flag:** "SouthCoast Logistics" and "Southern Logistics Pty Ltd" appear as two distinct vendors within the *same* budget document (`budget_1300_924`) for $158,333 and $80,000 respectively — plausibly two real vendors, but the near-identical names warrant a data-entry-duplication check.

**Vendors present in other departments' SGX records but ABSENT from Campaign's approved vendor list:**
- Red Centre Legal, Dingo Consulting (Legal invoices, $8,450 + $7,200 — see §2)
- SouthCoast Logistics, Southern Logistics Pty Ltd, EcoPackaging Australia (Finance/Ops budget lines)
- Wattle Accounting (engaged per HR/Legal staffing-discrepancy memo, 798/791)
- HiringMinds HR (named as an SGX partner in `project_update_1696_518`/`_583`, ids 701/583) — **no corresponding invoice or budget line anywhere in the corpus** — off-the-books engagement risk.
- Queensland Freight Co (named in Operations' `status_report_1748_311`, id 722, as directly supporting SGX logistics) — no invoice/budget line found.
- Perth Tech Solutions (named in Operations roster `roster_1310_811`, id 613, tied explicitly to Spring Glow) — no invoice/budget line found.
- Tasmanian IT, GoldCoast Marketing (named in Legal's `status_report_1464_849`/`_v1`, 181/975, as SGX contract-review subjects) — absent from Campaign's own budget documents.

---

## 4. RECONCILED PROGRAM-TOTAL VARIANCE — SHOW OF WORK

**All candidate "total" figures found, in order:**
1. $54,200 — `spring_glow_budget.csv` (343) — Campaign creative-only line-item sum
2. $283,333 (incomplete, one row unseen) — `budget_1300_924` family (345/1002/180/352) — computed sum
3. $435,000 — `budget_1720_421` family (364/103/350) — stated "Total Budget Allocated"
4. $439,833 (computed; possibly $534,833 if a separate $95,000 advertising line is additive) — `budget_1750_589` family (346/1181/351)
5. $525,000 (budgeted) — `budget_0355_340.txt` (127)
6. $525,600 (actual spend) — `budget_0355_340_v1.md` (367)

**Collapsing to final variance** (using the two explicit, stated headline totals at the extremes — excluding the incomplete/computed figures which are not "stated" totals):
- Highest stated total: **$525,600** (`budget_0355_340_v1.md`, actual spend)
- Lowest stated total: **$54,200** (`spring_glow_budget.csv`)
- **Dollar gap: $525,600 − $54,200 = $471,400**
- **As % of highest figure: $471,400 / $525,600 = 89.7%**

If instead the computed $534,833 Finance/Ops figure is used as the ceiling, the gap widens to $480,633 (89.9% of highest) — materially the same conclusion: **the highest and lowest total figures found for SGX differ by roughly 90%**, meaning the low-end figure captures barely a tenth of what the high-end figure claims. This gap size itself is a finding: it indicates the $54,200 figure is a partial/creative-only budget being informally treated elsewhere as if it were the whole program.

---

## 5. PERSON / SIGNER CROSS-REFERENCE — DATA-INTEGRITY FLAGS

| Name | Appearances | Flag |
|---|---|---|
| **Sarah Chen** | (a) Preparer of `budget_1720_421` SGX budget, Northstar Media, 2026-01-28 (364/103/350); (b) "VP of Marketing Strategy," **Lumina Beauty Corp** (a different company entirely), author of `brief.txt` (610), dated 2023-10-26 | **High-confidence flag.** Same full name tied to two different employers, different titles, and a ~3-year date gap. Either coincidental namesake, cross-contaminated template, or fabricated authorship — the "Spring Glow" brief itself (Lumina Beauty Corp, GlowSerum, 25% uplift target) reads as the ancestor of Northstar's SGX campaign, suggesting document/IP cross-contamination between two supposedly unrelated companies. |
| **Marco Nguyen** vs. **Paulo Eriksen** | Both hold title "**Campaign Manager, Campaign Department**" on SGX in overlapping windows: Marco Nguyen — `meeting_notes_1150_752` (2025-10-28, id 433) and `invoice_0091_116` (2026-06-05, id 491); Paulo Eriksen — `project_update_1696_518` (2026-05-29, id 701) | **Flag.** Two different individuals claim the identical single-owner title on the same project in the same period, with no transition memo found. Org-chart inconsistency or fabricated authorship. |
| **Paulo Silva** vs. **Isla Carmichael** | Both hold identical title "**Brand Strategist, Campaign Department**": Paulo Silva — `memo_0764_205` (2025-08-10, id 727); Isla Carmichael — `invoice_0203_288` (2026-05-07, id 260) | Lower-confidence flag — plausible if the team has multiple brand strategists, but consistent with a pattern of reused boilerplate author/title blocks across documents. |
| **Owen Yamamoto** (IT Manager) | `ticket_0949_378.txt` (base file) dated 2026-08-06 vs. `ticket_0949_378_v1.md` (the "_v1") dated **2025-08-06** | **Flag — reversed versioning.** Elsewhere in the corpus, "_v1" files consistently carry a date exactly +1 year later than their base file (e.g., invoice 0203_288, invoice 0091_116, memo 0764_205, budget 1720_421 all show base-year → base-year+1 in _v1). Here the direction is *reversed* (base = 2026, _v1 = 2025), proving the date field is not a reliable version-history indicator anywhere in this system — it appears to be arbitrarily/synthetically altered rather than genuinely sequential. |
| **NorthPac Legal invoice numbering** | `invoice_0091_116.md` — "Document Date: 2026-06-05," Invoice Number **INV-2026-06-017**; `invoice_0091_116_v1.txt` — "Document Date: 2027-06-05," same Invoice Number **INV-2026-06-017** | **Flag.** The v1's stated document date (2027) is inconsistent with the invoice number it reuses (embeds "2026"). Signature/number block reads as pasted from the base file rather than independently generated — classic template-reuse/fraud signature. |
| **Isla Carmichael** invoice numbering | `invoice_0203_288.md` — Invoice No. NMS-INV-**2026**-05-07-012; `invoice_0203_288_v1.txt` — Invoice No. NMS-INV-**2027**-05-07-012, both stamped "Date: 2026-05-07" | Same pattern as above — invoice number year and stated date disagree in the v1 copy. |

**Systemic conclusion for Section 5:** The recurring "+1 year in the _v1 file, but embedded IDs left unchanged" pattern, plus one confirmed reversed-direction exception, indicates these are not legitimate version-control artifacts but either (a) a corrupted ingestion/versioning pipeline, or (b) deliberately altered duplicate records — both of which should be treated as a fraud-risk finding in their own right, independent of any single dollar amount.

---

## SOURCE CITATION INDEX (key documents used)

- `spring_glow_budget.csv` (343) — creative-only budget, $54,200
- `budget_1720_421.txt` (364), `.md` (103), `_v1.md` (350) — $435,000 total, Sarah Chen
- `budget_1300_924.md` (345)/`.csv` (1002, 180)/`_v1.csv` (352) — SouthCoast Logistics $158,333, Southern Logistics $80,000, CRM $45,000
- `budget_1750_589.md` (346)/`.csv` (1181)/`_v1.csv` (351) — Skyline Analytics $158,333, EcoPackaging $8,500, distribution $15,000, CRM $48,000, salaries $210,000
- `budget_0355_340.txt` (127)/`_v1.md` (367) — $525,000 budget / $525,600 actual, Quinn Reyes
- `budget_0446_702.md` (359) — Kangaroo Designs $15,800, NorthPac Legal $7,200 (Campaign ledger)
- `invoice_0203_288.md` (260)/`_v1.txt` (495) — Red Centre Legal $8,450, Dingo Consulting $7,200
- `invoice_0091_116.md` (491)/`_v1.txt` (490) — NorthPac Legal $12,450
- `invoice_1347_984.csv` (89)/`_v1` (1303) — Red Centre Legal $4,850, EcoPackaging $3,200, Aurora Research $5,750, all Pending
- `invoice_0329_735.csv` (1180)/`.md` (257)/`_v1.csv` (497) — Aussie Web Solutions/Red Centre Legal, Pending/Draft/Unpaid
- `memo_0764_205.txt` (727)/`_v1` (401) — TVC hold, color-gradient issue
- `memo_0533_432.md` (798)/`_v1.txt` (791) — staffing count discrepancies
- `ticket_0949_378.txt` (223)/`_v1.md` (212); `ticket_1999_202.md` (214)/`_v1.csv` (207) — IT risk items
- `status_report_1464_849.md` (181)/`_v1` (975) — Legal vendor-checklist status
- `project_update_1696_518.md` (701)/`_v1` (583) — HiringMinds HR, Aurora Research references
- `status_report_1748_311.md` (722) — Queensland Freight Co / Logistics Optimization Project
- `roster_1310_811.md` (613) — Perth Tech Solutions
- `meeting_notes_1150_752.md` (433) — 7% budget variance note, Aurora Research/Kangaroo Designs onboarding
- `brief.txt` (610) — Lumina Beauty Corp "Sarah Chen" cross-reference flag

**Caveat:** Several items above (rows 5–8 in the risk register, EcoPackaging invoice reconciliation) were matched to SGX by vendor overlap rather than an explicit project tag in the retrieved excerpt; these should be confirmed against full source records before being treated as certain SGX exposures.
